enriched_points.expanded_content: Satellite remote sensing enables efficient monitoring of vegetation health through indices like NDVI, revealing variations in plant vitality across landscapes over time.
Remote sensing techniques allow for real-time tracking of water quality parameters, including chlorophyll levels and turbidity, essential for managing aquatic ecosystems.
Satellite data assists in analyzing climate variables such as temperature and precipitation patterns, crucial for understanding climate change impacts on ecosystems.
Remote sensing contributes to monitoring and achieving Sustainable Development Goals, particularly SDG15, by providing critical data on ecosystem health and biodiversity.
